steps.title: Solve the second inequality for y
Combine with the first inequality
steps.content: Since $y < 42 - 7x$ and $y > 70$, any solution must satisfy $70 < y < 42 - 7x$. For this to be possible, the upper bound must exceed 70: $42 - 7x > 70$.

steps.title: Eliminate choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$x > 6$': The inequality sign is wrong direction and the value is wrong. ✗
  • Choice B — '$x < 6$': The direction is correct but the threshold 6 is wrong. ✗
  • Choice C — '$x > -4$': The threshold -4 is right but the inequality sign is reversed. ✗
  • Choice D — '$x < -4$': Matches $-7x > 28 \Rightarrow x < -4$. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
